item_comparisons.reviewer_note: Automated draft review: question part (b) asks for r and z^3 in re^{i theta} form; mark scheme part (b) uses modulus/argument method. Primary P3 complex arithmetic/modulus/argument/polar-form skill is confirmed for subpart b. P1 trigonometry is supporting context only. This is an automated draft selection saved from the visual review packet; it is not reviewed registry evidence.

Automated draft review: part (c) explicitly requires use of an iterative formula and the mark scheme awards iteration steps and convergence justification. Fixed-point iteration is the primary skill; root-location context is supporting. This is an automated draft selection saved from the visual review packet; it is not reviewed registry evidence.

Automated draft adjustment: part (b) asks for the exact area of a region and the mark scheme uses integration with trig identities. The suggested trig-identities skill is supporting method context, not the safest exact target; likely needs routing toward integration/area evidence before clean use. This is an automated draft selection saved from the visual review packet; it is not reviewed registry evidence.

Automated draft review: the question uses parametric equations and part (b) applies dy/dx from part (a) to the normal at t = -1. Mark scheme confirms dx/dt, dy/dt and normal-gradient work. Parametric differentiation is confirmed for subpart b. This is an automated draft selection saved from the visual review packet; it is not reviewed registry evidence.

Automated draft review: part (b) finds a point on a 3D line by perpendicular projection and extends a vector ratio. Mark scheme uses vector-line equations and scalar product/perpendicularity. Vector lines skill is confirmed for subpart b. This is an automated draft selection saved from the visual review packet; it is not reviewed registry evidence.

Automated draft review: part (c) gives an iterative formula and asks for iterations to determine a. Mark scheme awards iteration to 4 d.p. and final 2 d.p. value. Fixed-point iteration is confirmed for subpart c. This is an automated draft selection saved from the visual review packet; it is not reviewed registry evidence.

Automated draft review: part (c) requires using the given iterative formula to calculate the root with each iteration. Mark scheme confirms iterative formula use and final accuracy. Fixed-point iteration is confirmed for subpart c. This is an automated draft selection saved from the visual review packet; it is not reviewed registry evidence.

Automated draft review: whole question is a remainder theorem / polynomial division setup with two remainders to solve for constants. Mark scheme substitutes x=-2 and x=1/2 or uses polynomial division. Whole-question polynomial division/factor-remainder skill is confirmed. This is an automated draft selection saved from the visual review packet; it is not reviewed registry evidence.

Automated draft review: part (d) asks for the greatest value of arg z on an Argand locus; mark scheme uses argument geometry. Complex modulus/argument skill is confirmed for subpart d. This is an automated draft selection saved from the visual review packet; it is not reviewed registry evidence.

Automated draft review: part (c) explicitly uses an iterative formula to determine the root and mark scheme awards iteration outputs. Fixed-point iteration is confirmed for subpart c. This is an automated draft selection saved from the visual review packet; it is not reviewed registry evidence.

Automated draft review: whole item is parametric differentiation, requiring dx/dtheta, dy/dtheta, quotient-rule differentiation and dy/dx. Parametric differentiation is confirmed for whole question. This is an automated draft selection saved from the visual review packet; it is not reviewed registry evidence.

Automated draft adjustment: the selected subpart (c) only asks for limiting behaviour from the differential-equation solution; the main method in part (b) is separable differential equations with logarithms. Log/exponential equations are supporting algebra, not a clean exact target for subpart c alone. This is an automated draft selection saved from the visual review packet; it is not reviewed registry evidence.

Automated draft adjustment: the mark scheme differentiates an implicit curve and solves a tangent-gradient condition. The suggested derivative-rules skill is supporting; the safer exact P3 target is parametric/implicit differentiation. Do not promote as derivative-rules evidence without retagging. This is an automated draft selection saved from the visual review packet; it is not reviewed registry evidence.

Automated draft review: whole item asks for a binomial expansion of (3+x)(1-2x)^{1/2} up to x^2. Mark scheme confirms binomial expansion coefficients and multiplication. P3 rational binomial expansion is confirmed. This is an automated draft selection saved from the visual review packet; it is not reviewed registry evidence.

Automated draft adjustment: although the queue action says defer_ambiguous_skill, part (d) explicitly uses the iterative formula x_{n+1}=ln(3+x_n) to calculate the root. Mark scheme confirms iteration and final root. Fixed-point iteration is confirmed for subpart d. This is an automated draft selection saved from the visual review packet; it is not reviewed registry evidence.

Block this candidate unless retagged to parametric/implicit differentiation. This is an automated draft selection saved from the visual review packet; it is not reviewed registry evidence.

Automated draft review: part (c) finds the intersection of two 3D lines using vector equations. Mark scheme confirms line equations and solving parameters. Automated draft review: whole item solves an exponential inequality involving 2^{x+1}; mark scheme confirms logarithmic/exponential inequality solution. fields_safe_for_advisory_indexing: canonical question and mark-scheme asset refs for reviewer navigation when asset validation passes.
topic-routing alignment and confidence for triage buckets, with strict-filter safety kept separate.
Content Lab generation_gate block reasons for review prioritization.
candidate primary/supporting skill IDs for reviewer packets only, not mastery or generation authority.
part/subpart labels and advisory mark-event part_path for choosing what to inspect, not for automatic promotion.
fields_that_need_stricter_gating: reviewed_source_skill_ids: keep empty or blocked until reviewer resolves exact skill; 4/25 draft responses rejected the automated exact skill.
mark_event_refs: keep advisory-only out of generation readiness; all selected records still had unreviewed mark events.
supporting_candidate_skill_ids: keep as context only; repeated rejected records were supporting-method matches rather than safe exact targets.
topic_routing_alignment=supporting_topic: treat as a review-risk signal, not a strict routing permission.
decomposition candidates: keep part boundaries review-gated because 4/25 needed adjustment and 4 whole-question items did not need decomposition.
improvements_actually_implemented: Added this reviewed-batch conclusion JSON/Markdown report builder.
Added validator warnings for clean records with unreviewed mark-event refs.
Added validator warnings for clean records with unverified source question or mark-scheme asset refs.
Added targeted tests for the new conclusion report and validator diagnostics.
improvements_intentionally_deferred: No candidate promotion from draft responses into the reviewed registry.
No automatic topic-routing or skill-routing changes from this small batch.
No difficulty/index/band logic changes; this batch did not carry reviewed difficulty evidence.
No Content Lab generation-readiness or mastery-readiness change.
next_recommended_review_batch_or_validat: Convert a small subset of the 21 clean draft responses into validated reviewed-decision registry records with reviewer identity, timestamps, reviewed regions, and verified asset refs.
Run a targeted retag review for the 4 rejected/ambiguous records before they re-enter candidate batches.
Review fixed-point iteration and complex-number subpart candidates next, because those were the strongest repeated clean patterns in this draft batch.
Rerun or repair the topic-routing failure batches that used unavailable evidence labels before claiming strict-filter safety.
risks_and_concerns: The completed response file is still marked human_review_response_draft and repeatedly says it is not registry evidence.
The sample is only 25 P3 exact-skill candidates, all selected from cross-topic-reviewable records.
There are zero clean validated reviewed-registry records, so runtime authority remains unavailable.
Topic-routing strict-filter safety is still false in the asset validation summary because 153 topic-routing records failed.
Part-level review currently relies on whole-question and whole mark-scheme images for many records.
specific_recommended_pipeline_improvemen: Add a reviewed-batch conclusion report so future decisions cite reviewed records and draft status explicitly.
Surface repeated failure modes: supporting-method skill mistaken as exact target, implicit/parametric differentiation retag needs, and integration-area questions routed through trig identities.
Warn when clean reviewed-decision records still carry unreviewed mark events, because those records cannot support candidate generation.
Warn when clean records lack verified asset-ref flags, so image inspection and asset metadata stay aligned.
Keep Content Lab blocked when source skill IDs are missing or mark events are not reviewed.
top_failure_modes: Automated exact skill rejected or blocked on `4` draft records.
Supporting method mistaken for possible target skill on `4` draft records.
`supporting_topic` alignment appeared in `3` of the rejected/ambiguous draft records.

Observed retag needs: trig identity used inside integration-area work, log/exponential algebra inside differential-equation work, derivative-rules label on implicit differentiation, and polynomial label on stationary implicit-differentiation work.
top_reliable_signals: Primary candidate skill matched draft review on `21` records; strongest repeated patterns were `9709_p3_3_9_complex_arithmetic_polar_form` (6), `9709_p3_3_6_fixed_point_iteration` (5), `9709_p3_3_3_identities_compound_double_angle_equations` (3), `9709_p3_3_4_parametric_implicit_differentiation` (2).
Canonical asset refs were present for all selected records and supported reviewer navigation.
Part/subpart scoping was useful for triage: most draft-clean records were subpart-level, while four were whole-question safe.
Content Lab gate reasons were reliable safety diagnostics: all selected records remained blocked by unreviewed mark events and missing source skill IDs.
Advisory mark-event status was consistent across the template: `true`.
warning: The batch response file is review evidence for this report only. It is marked as draft notes and is not the validated reviewed-decision registry.
